sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths (2005) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and mystery, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven, Case Closed: The Fist of Blue Sapphire—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths (2005) is best described as Animation, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: Strategy Above the Depths part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
